_draft_note: DRAFT for clinical review (low_confidence content ratification, 2026-07-07). NOT loaded (docs/, not src/.../templates/). This draft ratifies as APPROVED CONTENT the behaviour-frozen text already merged as live low_confidence.json v1.0.0 via PR #124 (the composer-migration eng PR). It is NOT an engagement expansion: per the batch steer, low_confidence keeps its short clarifying contract because its single clarifying QUESTION is already the bridge — do NOT add a second bridge here. PROMOTE on sign-off: set approved_by + status='approved' on the LIVE src template (content is identical, so no code change; this closes the approved_by:null governance gap for this surface).
content: INTENT: The user's message was ambiguous and it is not clear what they need. Emotional intensity: {intensity}/10. Ask one gentle, open ended clarifying question to understand better. Be warm and non judgmental. Do not assume what they need. Keep your reply to at most two sentences.
_clinical_review_note: SAFETY-COUPLED: the two-sentence cap is what keeps the suppressed freeflow guardrail benign on this path (a 2-sentence clarifying reply cannot deliver a structured protocol as prose; composer.py override-suppression). If clinical review wants to relax the cap, the freeflow guardrail must be re-enabled on the low_confidence override path in the same change. One gentle clarifying question only; it is the bridge. No affect-assumptive language. No em dashes.
